metric_value: lead consumer IT market including access PCs imaging products and new consumer electronics
notes: HP's stated corporate strategy at time of study was to lead consumer IT market; analyst frames the study around this strategic claim

metric_value: worked well for 6 months
notes: Camera installed in May 2001 under Windows ME; photo quality consistently high; transfer process straightforward for approximately 6 months

metric_value: missing .dll file prevented use as primary image-handling program
notes: ACDSee image display software bundled with C315 had a missing .dll file and was not usable; analyst worked around it without calling support

metric_value: major incompatibility after in-place upgrade to Windows XP
notes: In-place upgrade to Windows XP in October 2001 caused camera and HP software to fail; photos could not be transferred

metric_value: website claim of native XP support technically correct but misleading
notes: HP website stated C315 has native XP support; true only for clean install or factory-installed XP; not true for in-place upgrade which Microsoft recommended

metric_value: HP photo transfer software incompatible with Windows XP
notes: HP application software that ships with camera interferes with XP native photo transfer; incompatibility not documented on support site or known by support staff

metric_value: single tech support call cost HP more than retail price of camera
notes: Based on Aberdeen Group industry help-desk research conducted over several years; single 45-minute call exceeded camera retail cost

metric_value: 45 minutes
notes: HP support technician resolved immediate problem in approximately 45 minutes; analyst rated technician highly for listening and triage skills

metric_value: HP documentation failed entirely to address XP compatibility for in-place upgrade users
notes: Solution (do not install HP photo transfer software) not documented anywhere on HP support site; support technician also unaware of the issue

metric_value: cannot afford many support calls on mass-market products and remain profitable
notes: Analyst invokes economic law of consumer electronics: high support call volume destroys margins on mass-market products; HP violated this principle

metric_value: HP has a lot of work ahead to achieve world-class leadership in consumer IT
notes: Analyst concludes HP must fix internal process problems simplify instructions and consumer support or lose consumer market to competitors

metric_value: programs and devices that worked with prior Windows may not work with XP
notes: General consumer warning that Windows XP introduced enough changes to break existing hardware and software compatibility even for supported products

metric_value: dissatisfied consumers will go elsewhere guaranteeing HP loses race for consumer mind and market share
notes: Analyst predicts HP will cede consumer IT market to competitors and face millions in support costs if improvements not made quickly

metric_value: HP became major consumer IT player then split into HP Inc. and Hewlett Packard Enterprise in November 2015
notes: Retrospective outcome: HP did become significant consumer IT and imaging company; company eventually split in 2015 separating consumer/printing from enterprise divisions

metric_value: Windows XP became most successful Windows version; reached end-of-life April 2014 after 12+ years
notes: Retrospective outcome: Windows XP overcame early compatibility issues and became dominant OS for over a decade; EOL April 8 2014

metric_value: consumer digital cameras largely replaced by smartphone cameras by approximately 2015
notes: Retrospective outcome: smartphone cameras displaced dedicated consumer digital cameras; the HP C315 product category essentially ceased to be a mass-market segment

metric_value: consistently high quality photographs
notes: Analyst noted that despite software problems photos taken with C315 were consistently of high quality over 6 months of use

metric_value: ROM upgrade failed due to XP disk formatting process corrupting camera digital media
notes: Camera software detected available ROM update during reinstall; update failed because Windows XP formatting attempt had changed camera digital media state

metric_value: many unhappy HP camera owners who upgrade to Windows XP plus bundled PC+camera purchasers at risk
notes: Analyst projects the undocumented incompatibility will affect many consumers beyond himself; especially those buying bundled HP PC+camera who follow camera installation instructions

metric_value: consumers need simpler instructions than IT professionals and have higher support expectations and lower pain tolerance
notes: Analyst recommendation to HP: consumer documentation and support must be qualitatively different from enterprise IT support; not just simplified IT documentation
